name: RamanBioLib — Reference Raman Spectra of Biomolecules
description: Reference Raman spectra (450–1800 cm⁻¹, 1 cm⁻¹ resolution) of ~140 pure biomolecules including amino acids, nucleotides, lipids, and sugars. Each spectrum is labelled by biomolecule name. Useful for spectral assignment and as a reference library for classification benchmarks.
citeAs: @article{TERAN2025105476, title = {Open Raman spectral library for biomolecule identification}, journal = {Chemometrics and Intelligent Laboratory Systems}, volume = {264}, pages = {105476}, year = {2025}, issn = {0169-7439}, doi = {https://doi.org/10.1016/j.chemolab.2025.105476}, url = {https://www.sciencedirect.com/science/article/pii/S0169743925001613}, author = {Marcelo Terán and José Javier Ruiz and Pablo Loza-Alvarez and David Masip and David Merino}, keywords = {Raman spectroscopy, Spectral library, Biomolecules, Biomedicine, Database, Open-source}}

distribution.name: RamanBioLib — Reference Raman Spectra of Biomolecules
distribution.description: Source data hosted at the original repository.

field.name: spectrum_id
field.description: Sequential integer index identifying each spectrum.

field.name: raman_shift_cm_inv
field.description: Raman shift wavenumber axis in cm⁻¹. Shape: (n_wavenumbers,).

field.name: spectrum_intensity
field.description: Raman scattering intensity in arbitrary units. Shape: (n_wavenumbers,) per spectrum.

field.name: target
field.description: Class label: string identifier of the sample class.

rai:dataCollection: Raman spectra were acquired experimentally using a laser spectrometer. Each spectrum represents the scattered light intensity as a function of Raman shift (cm⁻¹).
rai:dataPreprocessingProtocol: Spectra are provided with class labels. Any preprocessing applied (baseline correction, smoothing, normalisation) is described in the source publication.
rai:dataAnnotationProtocol: Class labels assigned based on known sample identity or validated analytical reference methods. Labels were assigned by domain experts or clinical diagnosis; no crowdsourced annotation was used.
rai:personalSensitiveInformation: No personal or sensitive information is included. All spectra originate from chemical, biological, or material samples without individual human identifiers.
rai:dataBiases: Data were acquired under controlled laboratory conditions and may not generalise to field or clinical settings. Spectral profiles are influenced by instrument-specific factors (laser wavelength, detector type, optical path length, integration time). Class imbalances may be present in classification datasets.
rai:dataLimitations: Spectral signatures are process- and instrument-specific; calibration transfer to other setups requires additional validation. Note: No explicit license was stated by the original data providers; CC BY 4.0 has been assumed. Users should verify the applicable terms with the original authors.
rai:dataSocialImpact: Enables the development of rapid, inline bioprocess monitoring tools that can reduce waste, improve yield, and lower costs in biomanufacturing and biotechnology.
rai:dataUseCases: Spectral library matching, material identification, disease screening, compound recognition, quality control. Not recommended for direct clinical diagnosis without professional validation and regulatory approval.
prov:wasGeneratedBy: Data collection: Laboratory measurements on biological materials including microorganisms, cell cultures, fermentation broths, and plant material. Preprocessing: Spectra are provided with class labels. Any preprocessing applied (baseline correction, smoothing, normalisation) is described in the source publication. Annotation: Class labels assigned based on known sample identity or validated analytical reference methods.
